48: export function useOrders() {
49:   return useQuery({
50:     queryKey: ["/api/orders"],
51:     queryFn: async () => {
52:       const res = await fetch("/api/orders", { credentials: "include", headers: getAuthHeaders() });
53:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Failed to fetch orders");
54:       return res.json() as Promise<Order[]>;
55:     },
56:   });
57: }
58: 
59: export function useOrder(id: number) {
60:   return useQuery({
61:     queryKey: ["/api/orders", id],
62:     queryFn: async () => {
63:       const res = await fetch(`/api/orders/${id}`, { credentials: "include", headers: getAuthHeaders() });
64:       if (res.status === 404) return null;
65:       if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Failed to fetch order");
66:       return res.json() as Promise<Order>;
67:     },
68:     enabled: !!id,
69:   });
70: }
71: 
72: export function useCreateOrder() {
73:   const queryClient = useQueryClient();
74:   
75:   return useMutation({
76:     mutationFn: async (data: { tableNumber: string; items: OrderItem[]; totalAmount: number; customerName?: string; phone?: string; notes?: string }) => {
77:       const res = await fetch("/api/orders", {
78:         method: "POST",
79:         headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json", ...getAuthHeaders() },
80:         body: JSON.stringify({
81:           ...data,
82:           status: "Pending",
83:           paymentStatus: "Unpaid",
84:         }),
85:         credentials: "include" });
86:       
87:       if (!res.ok) {
88:         const errorData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
89:         throw new Error(errorData.message || "Failed to create order");
90:       }
91:       
92:       return res.json();
93:     },
94:     onSuccess: () => {
95:       queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["/api/orders"] });
96:     },
97:   });
98: }

100: export function useUpdateOrder() {
101:   const queryClient = useQueryClient();
102:   
103:   return useMutation({
